text: Here we see visualisation of the thymoma.
And the phrenic nerve is identified and traced from the cranial aspect of the mass down to the inferior pole
The mediastinal pleura is then opened, taking great care not to injure the phrenic nerve.
And the opening is extended both cranially and all the way to the inferior aspect of the thymoma.
Once completed, the plane between the pericardium and the thymus can be developed with a mixture of blunt dissection and application of the ligasure device.
The plane and mediastinal attachments are divided again, both in a cranial direction, all the way to the inferior aspect of the thymic mass.
Once completed, the thymus is mobile and can be retracted inferiorly to reveal the thymic veins, which can be dissected before being divided with the ligature device.
Here we see inferior retraction of the thymoma
revealing a thymic vein which is then divided with the ligature.
The remainder of the mediastinal attachments of the thymoma are then divided, taking care not to injure the contralateral phrenic nerve.
and the specimen is then retrieved using the endocatch.
and the specimen can then be easily removed from the chest.
After inspection of the thymic bed, a 24 French chest drain is inserted.
and its position confirmed under direct vision to ensure its lie in the thymic bed.
The lung is then inflated under direct vision.
